reasoning: There is currently no approved vaccine for Lymphatic filariasis (Elephantiasis). Recent news from March 2024 indicates that a team at the University of Illinois Chicago received a grant to prepare their vaccine candidate for Phase 1 human trials, which they note would become the very first human vaccine for the disease if successful. Because clinical trials and regulatory approval typically take several years to complete, there is no realistic chance of a vaccine being successfully developed, approved, and added to the Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' by October 2025. I assign probabilities near the minimum bound, slightly increasing for later dates as horizon uncertainty grows.
